raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the core concept of unsupervised anomaly detection for self-preference bias and highlights where the draft drifts from this concept, proposing specific reframings.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks through the argument chain end-to-end, flagging every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., supervised threshold calibration) and proposing minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence levels and identifies missing evidence, such as prior work checks and baseline comparisons.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed dimensions without any drift, addressing all points within the active set.",
    "RX.4": "The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential issues in the original text, such as the supervised threshold calibration.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with a source (e.g., experimental protocol) and reason for the change, ensuring traceability."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.95,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.95,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the core concept of unsupervised anomaly detection for self-preference bias and highlights where the draft drifts from this concept, proposing specific reframings.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks through the argument chain end-to-end, flagging every load-bearing inconsistency (e.g., supervised threshold calibration) and proposing minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence levels and identifies missing evidence, such as prior work checks and baseline comparisons.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed dimensions without any drift, addressing all points within the active set.
rationale.RX.4: The agent avoids introducing new errors or harmful content and identifies potential issues in the original text, such as the supervised threshold calibration.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with a source (e.g., experimental protocol) and reason for the change, ensuring traceability.
